message:
feat(abi,core): implement atoll, strtoll, strtoull, strtoimax, strtoumax, secure_getenv

Expand stdlib ABI surface with six new symbols that real-world programs
depend on, closing gaps that caused GlibcCallThrough fallback:

- atoll: delegates to existing atol, returning c_longlong
- strtoll/strtoull: thin wrappers over strtol/strtoul with c_longlong/
  c_ulonglong return types, preserving endptr semantics
- strtoimax/strtoumax: full membrane-validated implementations with
  independent runtime_policy::decide cycles, scan_c_string bounds
  probing, and endptr writability checks -- matching the pattern
  established by strtol/strtoul but returning intmax_t/uintmax_t
- secure_getenv: GNU extension that returns NULL when uid!=euid or
  gid!=egid (setuid/setgid context), preventing environment variable
  leakage during privilege transitions

Core conversion layer (frankenlibc-core) gains corresponding pure-Rust
impl/helper functions (atoll, strtoll_impl, strtoimax_impl, strtoull_impl,
strtoumax_impl) that delegate to the existing strtol_impl/strtoul_impl,
with unit tests verifying i64::MIN/MAX and u64::MAX boundary values.

The mod.rs re-export list is updated to expose all new conversion functions.

Integration tests in stdlib_abi_test.rs validate the ABI entrypoints
end-to-end: atoll on i64 limits, strtoll/strtoull endptr positioning
on partial parses with trailing non-digit characters.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
